key_insight: A pan-cancer foundation model using immune concepts can predict immunotherapy response across multiple cancers and checkpoint drugs while also showing biologically interpretable mechanisms.
clinical_relevance: This could help match patients to immunotherapy and support early-phase trial selection, especially when existing biomarkers do not transfer well across tumor types or treatments.
method: Trained a concept-bottleneck transformer on bulk tumor transcriptomes.
Encoded expression into 44 biologically grounded immune concepts.
Used transfer learning to generalize across cancers, drugs, and clinical settings.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,184 tumors across 33 cancer types and evaluated in 16 independent cohorts spanning seven cancers and six checkpoint inhibitors, outperforming 22 baselines.
watch_item: Results are promising but prospective validation is still needed before routine clinical use, especially to confirm performance in new sites and patient populations.
paper_title: Generalizable AI predicts immunotherapy outcomes across cancers and treatments
